commitMessage:
Replace Azure Easy Auth with Google token auth; add post ownership model

auth.py: validates Authorization: Bearer google_id_token via google-auth
schema.py: build_post() accepts optional author_email stored in frontmatter
function_app.py: on create stores author_email; on update/delete enforces
  ownership (legacy posts fall through to ALLOWED_WRITERS allowlist)
post.py: replace MSAL with Google InstalledAppFlow; CLI sends same header as web
requirements.txt: add google-auth; requirements-cli.txt: swap msal for google-auth-oauthlib
tests: patch require_auth directly; 20 tests passing including ownership/allowlist cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.summary: Azure Functions (Python) API that manages markdown blog posts stored as files in a GitHub repository. Authentication is handled via Google ID tokens; post ownership is enforced per-creator, with an allowlist controlling who can write.
content.description: A serverless write API for quixotry.me's blog. Posts are markdown files with YAML frontmatter committed directly to GitHub; the API sits in front of that storage layer.
features: Create, read, update, and delete blog posts via REST endpoints
Google ID token authentication (Authorization: Bearer header) — unified path for both web app and CLI clients
Per-post ownership: author_email stored in frontmatter; only the creator can edit or delete their posts
Allowlist-gated writes via ALLOWED_WRITERS environment variable
Legacy post support: posts without author_email can be edited by any allowlisted writer; first editor's email is stamped as author
Slug auto-generation from post title using python-slugify
Published/draft state toggled via frontmatter field

architecture.overview: Azure Functions v2 app (Python) deployed to a Consumption plan. Each HTTP trigger handles one REST operation. Post storage is GitHub — the API clones/reads/writes markdown files via the GitHub REST API (PyGithub). Auth uses google-auth to validate Google ID tokens server-side; no session state is maintained.
keyPoints: Storage is GitHub, not a database — each post is a .md file with YAML frontmatter in skarumbu/quixotry repo
Google ID token validation via google.oauth2.id_token.verify_oauth2_token against GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID env var
ALLOWED_WRITERS env var (comma-separated emails) gates POST; PUT/DELETE further enforce per-post ownership via author_email frontmatter field
No Azure Easy Auth — all auth is application-level; Microsoft identity provider is disabled on the Function App
python-frontmatter used to parse and serialise YAML + markdown body; python-slugify for URL-safe slug generation

dataFlow.label: Azure Function validates Google ID token
sublines: google-auth verifies token signature and audience

dataFlow.label: Ownership / allowlist check
sublines: POST: email must be in ALLOWED_WRITERS
PUT/DELETE: requester must match author_email (or be in allowlist for legacy posts)

dataFlow.label: GitHub API read/write
sublines: PyGithub reads or commits .md files to skarumbu/quixotry
File content is YAML frontmatter + markdown body

dataFlow.label: Response returned to client
sublines: JSON with post data on reads
